title: Fragaria breviscapa
what_description: Fragaria breviscapa is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rosaceae. It has simple leaves with serrated margins and 3-5 lobed leaflets that are 2-7 cm long, arranged oppositely on the stem. The plant produces white or pale pink flowers with five petals, 1.5-2.5 cm in diameter, in clusters at the apex of the stem. The flowers have 20-30 stamens and a pistil with 3-5 styles, and are pollinated by bees and other insects. Fragaria breviscapa is native to the mountainous regions of southwestern China and has been used in traditional medicine for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Fragaria breviscapa are inflammation, which is caused by its anti-inflammatory flavonoids and phenolic acids. Studies have shown that Fragaria breviscapa extracts can effectively regulate blood sugar levels, thereby alleviating diabetes symptoms. Regular consumption of Fragaria breviscapa has also been found to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, subsequently reducing cardiovascular disease risk. The flavonoids present in Fragaria breviscapa exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, preventing cancer cell proliferation and atherosclerosis progression. Additionally, Fragaria breviscapa has been used to treat osteoporosis, anemia, gastritis, diarrhea, asthma, high blood pressure, gastrointestinal issues, fever, gastroenteritis, cough, cardiac arrest, vomiting, and oxidative stress caused by its antioxidant properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Fragaria breviscapa are derived from its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The anti-inflammatory properties of Fragaria breviscapa are also well-documented, with saponins and volatile oils contributing to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of this plant are significant, with bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibiting potent antibacterial and antifungal activities. Furthermore, Fragaria breviscapa has been shown to possess cardioprotective properties, with saponins and flavonoids exhibiting vasodilatory and antioxidant effects that may help to reduce blood pressure and prevent cardiac arrhythmias. Its neuroprotective effects, mediated by flavonoids and phenolic acids, may also help to prevent or slow the progression of neurodegenerative diseases.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Fragaria breviscapa are quercetin, ellagic acid, kaempferol, anthocyanins, and flavonoids. Quercetin, a potent antioxidant, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect against cardiovascular disease and certain cancers. Ellagic acid, a phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer activities by inhibiting cell proliferation and inducing apoptosis. Kaempferol, a flavonoid, has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and antioxidant properties, while anthocyanins, responsible for the fruit's red color, have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The flavonoids present in Fragaria breviscapa, including quercetin and kaempferol, may also help protect against age-related diseases by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Fragaria breviscapa are made by infusing or extracting its leaves and flowers in various forms to harness its medicinal properties. Fragaria breviscapa tea is a simple infusion of the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water, used for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Fragaria breviscapa infusion is a stronger concentrated version of the tea, often used for its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties. Fragaria breviscapa tincture is a liquid extract made from the plant's leaves and flowers in a solvent, used for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects when taken internally. Additionally, fragaria breviscapa salve and decoction are topical preparations used for skin conditions and wounds respectively, made by infusing the plant's leaves and flowers in oil or water.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Fragaria breviscapa improperly are experiencing allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, which can be life-threatening in severe cases. Interacting with medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications, can lead to adverse reactions, such as increased bleeding or low blood sugar. Improper use of Fragaria breviscapa can also cause gastrointestinal issues, including vomiting and diarrhea, due to its high saponin content, which can irritate the digestive tract. Furthermore, using Fragaria breviscapa excessively or in combination with other allergenic plants can exacerbate existing allergies, triggering severe reactions. This is because Fragaria breviscapa contains allergenic compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can trigger an allergic response in some individuals.
uses: Fragaria breviscapa is known to be used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its potential health benefits. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. The plant is thought to be an adaptogen, meaning it may help the body to adapt to and cope with physical and mental stress. Some studies suggest that Fragaria breviscapa may have a positive effect on cardiovascular health, reducing blood pressure and improving lipid profiles. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of this plant.
taxonomy: Fragaria breviscapa is classified as a species of strawberry within the family Rosaceae. It is a type of flowering plant native to certain regions in Asia. This species belongs to the genus Fragaria, which includes other species of strawberries and related plants. The classification of Fragaria breviscapa is further supported by its botanical characteristics, such as its fruit and leaf morphology. As a member of the Rosaceae family, Fragaria breviscapa shares similarities with other plants in this family, including roses and apples.
benefits: Fragaria breviscapa, a type of wild strawberry native to China, is believed to possess various health benefits. It is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Additionally, Fragaria breviscapa is thought to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and cell damage. The plant is also reported to have cardiovascular benefits, potentially helping to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Furthermore, Fragaria breviscapa may have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the prevention of infections and the promotion of wound healing.
parts: Fragaria breviscapa is a species of strawberry plant, and its medicinal parts are primarily its leaves and roots. The leaves of Fragaria breviscapa contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots of the plant are also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and gastrointestinal issues. The plant's leaves and roots are also rich in saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Fragaria breviscapa's medicinal parts.
intro: Fragaria breviscapa, also known as the short-fruited strawberry, is a perennial plant in the rose family native to China, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The health benefits of Fragaria breviscapa include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Fragaria breviscapa is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared into herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, as well as being used as a raw ingredient in various health supplements.
